judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue is overshadowed by significant operating losses and a high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — substantial future growth expected.
Forward P/E 37.06x and PEG ratio 0.78x — valuation may be justified by expected growth.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — healthy short-term liquidity.
Total cash $439.70M vs Total debt $280.42M — manageable debt load with ample cash.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is not profitable on an operating basis.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — negative returns indicate poor profitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Debt/Equity 25.36 — high leverage relative to equity.
Last quarter EPS surprise -44.8% — significant miss on earnings.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is seeing some selling pressure, mentioned in headlines alongside other AI leaders, despite Mark Cuban urging a focus on foundational AI. While the company's core business may be relevant to the AI narrative, broader market sentiment and specific stock movements in the sector are creating headwinds. Traders should monitor the ongoing sector rotation and any specific news flow related to AAOI's product pipeline or customer adoption.
